message:
fix(grpc): reject invalid compression flags and fail explicitly on unsupported compression

Two hardening fixes in the gRPC codec:

1. Decoder now validates the compression flag byte strictly: only 0
   (uncompressed) and 1 (compressed) are accepted per the gRPC wire
   protocol spec. Previously any non-zero value was treated as
   compressed, which could mask protocol violations from misbehaving
   peers. Invalid flag values now return a GrpcError::Protocol error.

2. FramedCodec::encode_message now returns GrpcError::Compression when
   use_compression is true, since compression is not yet implemented.
   Previously it silently emitted uncompressed frames regardless of the
   compression setting, which violated the caller's expectation.

Tests added:
  - test_grpc_codec_rejects_invalid_compression_flag
  - test_framed_codec_with_compression_errors_on_encode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
